All right, this is a little bit of a bonus video for people like me who don't much care for tech stuff. So that's going to be the first little brief section of this video and then the second half of this video is going to be how to use slightly less Redneck methodologies to actually get a pretty good accurate way of tracking your ads using ClickFunnels without having to use particularly expensive tracking services. So without any further ado, let's go. All right, so what I mentioned earlier in our video about Facebook and how to read your stats and everything was that you could just create a funnel that's totally dedicated to Facebook and that's what I used to do before I had Hyros. So I literally started using Hyros in 2021. So I made it for, you know, since 1999 without using any fancy tracking software at all. So what I would do is I would just like clone a funnel, you know, so this is my Convert Book funnel right here, for example, and I would literally just come into ClickFunnels and I would clone my entire funnel. I would copy the whole thing and then I would just have this one funnel be only for Facebook traffic. So when I created my Facebook ads, I would send it to this page and this is literally, you know, these are the only way someone's going to get here is incoming from Facebook ads. So if I look and I just go like all the way back and I sometimes break ClickFunnels because they kind of want to drive you crazy with their tracking here. Hopefully it won't break it. I've cloned it several times as you can see because I don't know what I was doing that for. But as you can see, it's the same web page and I've literally just cloned the funnel, you know, so you do that and it ends up being that you're only sending traffic from Facebook or YouTube or whatever to this particular funnel and ClickFunnels and that in and of itself is like really, really good and just overall knowing whether your ads are working worth the damn. All right. So let me give you an example. All right. So I just googled UTM builder. All right. So this is something like Google has. So I'm just going to click the top thing here. So what you do is you come here and you get your in click funnels here. You get your opt-in page and incidentally, click funnels is a little different in the sense that notice that the page it resolves to that actually goes to is different from the one it shows. So you want to click this and like let it resolve to the actual weird looking page. So I'm going to copy that. I'm just going to click my website here. And then we can put whatever we want to here. All right. So I would put like the campaign ID, you know, I could call it whatever. Let's just say, you know, big test and then the campaign source. I would change everything else. I would just change to the audience name. All right. Because we're really just looking to see what audiences are working. So this would let's say the audience name was Brendan Bershaward, you know, I just say Brendan. That's not how it's spelled Brendan. All right. And so I hit copy. And I would just paste, paste, paste, paste. All right. And then so now what it gives me is this long ass link right here. All right. And so when I go to set up my Facebook ad, okay, instead of like going just to this web page by itself, I could paste this in my ad set where I was targeting Brendan Bershaward fans. And then if I went to click funnels, and I basically came over here to the stats and I had like, if I clicked on filters, and I had like any of these things like content, term, anything, if I just said Brendan, all right. And I hit apply filter, it would show me all the sales where that little field equals Brendan. Now you could try to get super granular and put, you know, different, you know, things like have multiple ads and each ad has its own URL and all that kind of stuff. But I don't bother with it because their stats are a little bit funky. But this is like pretty good. So this will allow you to overcome that delay that Facebook has and you can actually see which of your audiences are doing well by using this admittedly redneck methodology. Now, if you want to take it one step further, there is this program is a series as a set of scripts called CF powerscripts.com. All right, not power tools, power scripts made by this dude right here. And they've got it where it'll actually integrate Google Analytics with click funnels. And it's really, really cool. So what I'm going to do and hopefully this is cool with this dude is I'm actually going to splice in his tutorial on how to do that right here. So essentially that is taking what what his script is doing is taking that redneck version that I'm doing and taking it to a whole new level. So you don't actually have to clone your funnel, you know, for every single different ad source. So it's less redneck. I guess actually this isn't redneck at all. This is fancy. All right, so let's see if I can get this to import for us here. All right, we'll go full screen. Welcome to another CF powerscripts tutorial with me, Christian Reuchenwald. In this video, I'm going to show you how you can use CF powerscripts to add Google Analytics to your funnels in click funnels with just a few mouse clicks. In order to get started, you need to log into your CF powerscripts account. And once they're head to the powerscript section and either at the Google Analytics powerscript globally to all of your funnels at once, if you want to use the same Google Analytics tracking ID in all of them, or like I'm doing, pick the funnel where you want to add the script. Now once they're simply click on add new and look for the Google Analytics powerscript, click on it. And as you can see, all we need here is the Google Analytics tracking ID, which you can get when you're within your Google Analytics account and you click on the bottom left corner on admin, click on tracking info in the second column. And there click on tracking code. Now you will see the tracking ID in the top left corner and all you have to do is copy it, head back to CF powerscripts and enter the ID here. Now additionally, if you are within a European country or if you have to comply with GDPR, you might want to enable the anonymize IP option. This way Google Analytics is GDPR conform and you can click add script to add it to your funnels. Now if I click on the icon here to open the shortcuts and I open the live funnel step, now we can see once I enable Google Page Analytics Chrome extension that the Google Analytics tracking code has been found and that the code for this specific tracking ID has been loaded. That's it. That's how easy you can add Google Analytics tracking code to your funnels. Now if you were to use the same tracking ID in most of your funnels, you could add that one globally. And for the few funnels where you use a different tracking ID, you can just add it on funnel level separately and it will always use the most specific one. As always, if you have any questions whatsoever, take a look at our FAQ, maybe you'll already find an answer there.
